isolation.exclusiveAccess: Full exclusive access to backend for testing

assertions.title: Text-only algebra question end-to-end

steps.action: Send health check to verify backend is running

steps.expected: Receive structured response with correct answer (x=7), verification.status === 'passed', logical steps
steps.observed: Received response with finalAnswer='7' (correct), but verification.status='partial' instead of required 'passed'

steps.expected: finalAnswer contains '7', verification.status === 'passed', steps array non-empty with logical flow
steps.observed: finalAnswer='7' ✓, steps array has 4 logical steps ✓, verification.status='partial' ✗ (required: 'passed')

responseBody.problemSummary: Solve the linear equation 3x - 7 = 14 for the variable x.

steps.title: Setup the equation
steps.explanationMarkdown: We begin by identifying the linear equation provided in the problem statement. The goal is to isolate the variable x on one side of the equals sign using algebraic manipulation.

steps.title: Add constant term
steps.explanationMarkdown: To isolate the term with the variable, we must eliminate the constant on the left side. Adding 7 to both sides maintains equality and simplifies the path toward solving for x.

steps.title: Divide to isolate x
steps.explanationMarkdown: We now need to remove the coefficient multiplying x. By dividing both sides of the equation by 3, we utilize the multiplicative inverse property to find the exact value of the variable.

steps.title: Verify the solution
steps.explanationMarkdown: Substituting 7 back into the original equation confirms the validity of our result. Calculating 3 times 7 minus 7 yields 14, proving the left side matches the right side perfectly.

responseBody.conceptSummary: The core concept involves isolating a variable using inverse operations. Addition is used to move constants, and division is used to eliminate coefficients.

assertions.issues: FAIL: verification.status is 'partial' instead of required 'passed'. Root cause: The LLM is hallucinating and including a verification field in its response (even though not prompted to do so). The current fix in validateAndRepairLLMResponse() correctly handles the case where llmResponse.verification is undefined (doesn't copy it, allowing verifyMath() to run), but it DOES copy the verification field when the LLM provides one (line 66: verification: llmResponse.verification ? { ...llmResponse.verification } : undefined). This prevents verifyMath() from running because the condition 'if (!responseData.verification)' at line 313 evaluates to false. The fix needs to ALWAYS remove/ignore any verification field from the LLM response so that verifyMath() always generates it fresh.

summary: VAL-E2E-001 FAILED in Round 3. The verification repair override fix was partially implemented but incomplete. The fix correctly preserves undefined verification values (allowing verifyMath() to run), but it also preserves LLM-provided verification values, which prevents verifyMath() from running. The LLM is hallucinating a verification field with status='partial', and this is being copied through to the final response. Required fix: Always remove/ignore the verification field from LLM responses in validateAndRepairLLMResponse() so that verifyMath() always generates the verification result fresh using symbolic math computation.
